meta.setting: Telegraph lines, ridge roads, relay offices, and storm-cut poles around French Gulch
meta.rdr_clone_target: RDR-clone western with fragile communication, travel, law pressure, rumor, and hard choices

research_grounding.title: California State Parks Shasta State Historic Park
research_grounding.use: Northern California mining commerce, county infrastructure, and road-town context.

research_grounding.title: NPS Whiskeytown History and Culture
research_grounding.use: Regional settlement, roads, mining communities, and social pressure around communication.

generation_prompt.task: Generate the Wire Riders cast page for the 1899 Jefferson State western packet. This JSON contains shape and guidance only.
page_shape: square image reference immediately after the title
opening blockquote with invented line-repair note, relay warning, or cut-wire saying generated later
H2 archetype subtitle in the register of Telegraph Line Riders
one or two compact lore paragraphs introducing repair riders who carry news, lies, and the fragile thread of outside authority
H3 Role with a short table-facing function phrase
H3 Traits with two to four terse signs or habits
10 H4 headings, each followed later by a brief playable move, leverage, cost, edge, or complication
H3 Camp Say closing blockquote with plain meaning generated later
generation_guidance: Generate wire riders as practical technicians, riders, and messengers in a period when telegraph matters but distance still hurts.
Tie future moves to broken line, coded urgency, relay station trouble, weather damage, intercepted news, cut poles, dead batteries, and who gets told first.
Keep technology tactile and 1899-plausible: poles, insulators, pliers, line wire, notebooks, relay offices, wet saddles, and tired horses.
Let the page support rumor play, law heat, sabotage, rescue, and decisions about whether bad news should arrive.
Do not include final move names or final move text in this JSON.
generation_prompt.hand_cut_note: They should make the future look like a glass insulator cracked by hail. The wire can outrun a horse, but it cannot tell you who cut it.
generation_prompt.table_spark: A wire-rider scene should ask what message failed, who wanted silence, and whether the repair matters more than the truth.
must_preserve: Use 1899 telegraph and western road language only.
Keep communication fragile, physical, and socially dangerous.
The generated page must include image path cast/images/wire-riders.png.
No finished page prose, final moves, or final sayings belong in this prompt file.
avoid: do not mention any prior packet, inherited taxonomy, or non-western source language
do not include medieval, enchanted, sacred, or fake-tribal framing
do not use modern radio, telephone, power-grid, internet, or digital language
do not use modern slang, vehicles, plastic gear, or clean theme-park staging
self_check: The future markdown follows the exact heading sequence.
The future page is about telegraph repair, fragile news, road pressure, and table action.
The prompt contains no finished page prose.
All language belongs to grounded 1899 Jefferson State western play.
